concept.conceptName: Calcium [Moles/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration
Calcium [Mass/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration

en.name: Urinary calcium
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Urinary calcium is the concentration of calcium in a urine specimen — typically a spot (random) urine sample [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or as a mass concentration in mg/dL [2]. ## Included Concepts The set lists four LOINC "Lab Test" concepts measuring the calcium concentration in a spot urine specimen: - *3018133 / 2004-0 — Calcium [Moles/volume] in Urine (LOINC)*: spot (random) urine sample. Most widely used LOINC code. - *3006661 / 17862-4 — Calcium [Mass/volume] in Urine (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *42869545 / 71790-0 — Calcium [Moles/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration (LOINC)*: used when the source does not specify whether the sample is spot or timed. - *3034244 / 35675-8 — Calcium [Mass/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes and **`mg/dL`** (concept 8840) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts that exist for urinary calcium are deliberately not part of this set, grouped by reason for exclusion. ### Timed urine collections — different specimen (timed vs. spot) - *3024431 / 12511-2 — Calcium [Mass/volume] in 4 hour Urine* - *3017730 / 18488-7 — Calcium [Mass/volume] in 24 hour Urine* - *3009630 / 25362-5 — Calcium [Moles/volume] in 24 hour Urine* - *40760452 / 57334-5 — Calcium [Mass/volume] in 2 hour Urine* - *40760453 / 57335-2 — Calcium [Mass/volume] in 12 hour Urine* ### Excretion rates — different measurand (amount per unit time, not a concentration) - *3010838 / 14637-3 — Calcium [Moles/time] in 24 hour Urine* - *3007687 / 6874-2 — Calcium [Mass/time] in 24 hour Urine* - *3017170 / 18186-7 — Calcium [Mass/time] in 12 hour Urine* - *3041568 / 51610-4 — Calcium [Moles/time] in 12 hour Urine* - *3007614 / 16518-3 — Calcium [Mass/time] in 2 hour Urine* - *3017858 / 27091-8 — Calcium [Mass/time] in 4 hour Urine* - *3039921 / 53124-4 — Calcium [Moles/time] in 1 hour Urine* - *40762093 / 59000-0 — Calcium [Mass/time] in 18 hour Urine* - *3014721 / 32541-5 — Calcium [Molar amount] in Urine collected for unspecified duration* ### Ratios — different measurand (ratio to another analyte) - *3035982 / 13717-4 — Calcium/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in 24 hour Urine* - *3017754 / 24518-3 — Calcium/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in Urine* - *3023147 / 27037-1 — Calcium/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in 2 hour Urine* - *3030451 / 50839-0 — Calcium/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in 24 hour Urine* - *3002481 / 9321-1 — Calcium/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in Urine* - *3032454 / 49937-6 — Calcium/Oxalate [Molar ratio] in Urine* ### Qualitative — different measurand (presence, not a concentration) - *3012065 / 29276-3 — Calcium [Presence] in Urine* ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "urinary calcium", "urine Ca", "spot urine calcium" without further specification: `3018133 / 2004-0 — Calcium [Moles/volume] in Urine`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source unit is mg/dL**: convert to mmol/L using `1 mg/dL ≈ 0.2495 mmol/L`. - **Source unit is mEq/L**: convert to mmol/L using `1 mEq/L = 0.5 mmol/L` (calcium is divalent). - **Source reports a 24-hour (or other timed) urine collection**: do **not** map to this concept set. Timed urinary calcium is out of scope. - **Source reports a calcium-to-creatinine ratio**: do **not** map. These are different measurands. - **Do not confuse with**: calcium in blood (separate concept set, "Calcium in blood"), ionized calcium (separate concept set), urinary calcium excretion rate (mmol/24h), calcium/creatinine ratio. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2004-0 — Calcium [Moles/volume] in Urine. Available: https://loinc.org/2004-0/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
